name: Mocha Macadamia Decadence
recipeIngredient: 3 oz Bittersweet or semisweet chocolate; chopped
2 Teaspoons Granulated instant espresso - or coffee
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F. Butter an 8-inch round cake pan with 2-inch sides.
2. Line the bottom of the pan with parchment paper.
3. Stir chopped chocolates in a double boiler over simmering water until melted and smooth. Cool slightly.
4. Process macadamia nuts and flour in a food processor until the nuts are finely ground. Transfer nut mixture to a small bowl.
5. Add butter and sugar to the food processor and blend, turning off, until the mixture is smooth.
6. Add eggs, Cognac or brandy, instant espresso (or coffee), and vanilla extract to the food processor and blend until smooth.
7. Add melted chocolate to the food processor and blend until just combined.
8. Add nut mixture using on/off turns in the food processor until the mixture is just combined.
9. Transfer batter to the prepared pan. Smooth the top with a spatula.
10. Bake for 40 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted halfway between the edge and center of the cake comes out clean. Cool the cake in the pan on a rack for 30 minutes.
11. Invert the cake onto the rack; remove the parchment and cool.
2. For Glaze: Bring cream to a boil in a heavy small saucepan. Remove from heat. Add chocolate and stir until melted and smooth. Stir in corn syrup. Let glaze stand until cool enough to spread, about 20 minutes.
3. Transfer cake to a rack. Spread glaze over the top and sides of the cake. Refrigerate until set.
4. Transfer cake to a platter. Let stand at room temperature for 1 hour before serving.
